perturbation.original_text: 2.9. Compliance with Laws. To the extent practicable and not otherwise adversely affecting the rights of any party to this Agreement, the Parties shall use their commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that the Company-Skype Branded Content and Company-Skype Branded Web Site complies with the applicable laws and regulations of the PRC that are identified in writing by Online BVI and/or the Company and delivered to Skype in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.9. Compliance with Laws. To the extent deemed appropriate and not unduly affecting the rights of any party to this Agreement, the Parties may endeavor to ensure that the Company-Skype Branded Content and Company-Skype Branded Web Site nominally complies with vaguely applicable laws and regulations of the PRC that may be identified without obligation by Online BVI and/or the Company and casually mentioned to Skype at some point.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces significant ambiguity. 'To the extent deemed appropriate' makes compliance discretionary. 'Unduly affecting' is subjective. 'Nominally complies' suggests superficial compliance. 'Vaguely applicable' undermines the clarity of which laws apply. 'Without obligation' removes the requirement for Online BVI/Company to identify laws. 'Casually mentioned to Skype at some point' removes the requirement for written notice and introduces uncertainty about when notice is considered given. This ambiguity introduces a legal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 12, 24, 47 of the Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: The Cybersecurity Law of the PRC requires strict data security measures and compliance with regulations related to online content. The original clause mandates commercially reasonable efforts to comply with laws, whereas the modified text introduces broad discretion, undermining the obligation to meet specific requirements for cybersecurity and content control as required by PRC law. Specifically, Articles 12, 24 and 47 put obligations in place to enforce network operations, monitor network security, and control information dissemination.

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces multiple ambiguities that undermine the original obligation to comply with applicable PRC laws, creating a significant contradiction. The original text required 'commercially reasonable efforts' to ensure compliance, while the revised text makes compliance discretionary ('To the extent deemed appropriate') and reduces the standard to 'nominally complies' with 'vaguely applicable' laws. The 'without obligation' clause removes the responsibility to identify specific laws. Scraped Law Snippet 2 refers to the Cyber Security Law of 2016 which imposes cybersecurity obligations. The change significantly weakens the obligation to comply with PRC laws by making compliance subjective and optional, thus establishing a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3.1. The Company will, and Online BVI will cause the Company to, use its commercially reasonable efforts to promote the Company- Skype Branded Application and the Company-Skype Branded Web Site to all of Online BVI's customers in the Territory, as each is agreed by the Parties in accordance with Section 2.2 above. Subject to Section 2.3.2, all decisions relating to the specific promotional activities of the Company and the Online Group hereunder, including, without limitation, any advertising, licensing, distribution or other exploitation of the Company-Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Toolbar, Company-Skype Branded Content or Company-Skype Branded Web Site, shall be mutually agreed upon, provided, however, that the Online Group shall at a minimum (i) promote the availability of the Company-Skype Branded Application to all visitors of the Online BVI Web Site, (ii) promote the availability of the Company-Skype Branded Application to all its existing customers and/or users, provided that this obligation shall not require the Online Group to communicate with those customers and/or users by SMS or e-mail where prohibited by contract, applicable laws, regulations and/or policies of telecommunications operators, and (iii) place each of the following in a reasonably conspicuous position within a prominent home or next level Web Page, the "preferred partners" area, and any other area or other Web Pages within the Online BVI Web Site as the Parties may mutually agree to from time to time: (x) links to the Company-Skype Branded Application and a mutually-agreeable short profile of Skype; and (y) promotional material for the Company-Skype Branded Application and links to the Company-Skype Branded Web Site.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3.1. The Company might, and Online BVI might consider causing the Company to, vaguely promote the Company- Skype Branded Application and the Company-Skype Branded Web Site to some of Online BVI's customers in the Territory, as potentially considered by the Parties in accordance with Section 2.2 above. Subject to Section 2.3.2, all discussions relating to some of the promotional activities of the Company and the Online Group hereunder, including, without limitation, any advertising, licensing, distribution or other exploitation of the Company-Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Toolbar, Company-Skype Branded Content or Company-Skype Branded Web Site, may be occasionally reviewed, provided, however, that the Online Group shall at a minimum (i) perhaps mention the availability of the Company-Skype Branded Application to a few visitors of the Online BVI Web Site, (ii) mention the availability of the Company-Skype Branded Application to a selection of its existing customers and/or users, provided that this suggestion shall not require the Online Group to communicate with those customers and/or users by SMS or e-mail where reasonably avoidable under contract, seemingly applicable laws, general guidelines and/or potential expectations of telecommunications operators, and (iii) place each of the following in a somewhat noticeable position within a relatively accessible Web Page, the "partners" area, and some other area or other Web Pages within the Online BVI Web Site as the Parties might forgetfully acknowledge to from time to time: (x) links to the Company-Skype Branded Application and a short profile of Skype; and (y) promotional material for the Company-Skype Branded Application and links to the Company-Skype Branded Web Site.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the promotional obligations. 'Might' and 'might consider' make the obligations discretionary. 'Vaguely promote' lacks a clear standard. 'Potentially considered' introduces uncertainty about the agreement process. 'Some of the promotional activities,' 'occasionally reviewed,' 'perhaps mention,' 'a few visitors,' 'a selection of its existing customers,' 'seemingly applicable laws,' 'general guidelines,' 'potential expectations,' 'somewhat noticeable,' 'relatively accessible,' and 'might forgetfully acknowledge' all contribute to vagueness and weaken the original obligations. The cumulative effect contradicts advertising regulations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Advertising Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 4, 8, 9, 10, 11, 14, 24 of the Advertising Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: The Advertising Law of the PRC sets standards for truthful and effective advertising. Article 4 requires that advertising must be truthful and legal. The original clause requires a good-faith effort to promote the application, which aligns with these requirements. However, the modified text uses ambiguous language and discretionary terms like 'might vaguely promote,' undermining the requirement for truthful and effective promotion as per the Advertising Law, which may violate Articles 8, 9, 10, 11, 14, 24. The vague and discretionary nature of the promotion goes against the standard of care set forth in advertising requirements.
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes a clear obligation to promote the Company-Skype Branded Application and Web Site, using "commercially reasonable efforts." The modified text significantly weakens this obligation by using ambiguous and discretionary terms such as "might vaguely promote" and "potentially considered." This introduces uncertainty and makes the promotional obligation unenforceable, which contradicts the advertising standards that aims to be truthful and effective.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3.1 Within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, the Company will, and Online BVI will cause the Company to, furnish to Skype complete and accurate statements (each, a "Statement") in a form acceptable to Skype, certified to be accurate by an officer of the Company or Online BVI, as applicable, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by the Company or Online BVI, or if applicable, any Subsidiary or other Affiliate (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Tom Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue. In the event that Skype provides any Payment Services hereunder, within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, Skype shall furnish to the Company complete and accurate Statements in a form acceptable to Online BVI, certified to be accurate by an officer of Skype, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by Skype, or if applicable, its Subsidiaries or other Affiliates (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Skype Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue. The respective Parties shall use their best efforts to accompany each respective Statement with payment, to Skype, if provided by the Company or Online BVI (or their associated third parties set forth above), or to the Company (or Online BVI, as applicable), if provided by Skype (or its associated third parties set forth above), as applicable, of 50% of the Adjusted Net Revenue shown on the respective Statements. Provided that the respective Parties utilize their best efforts to provide such payments together with the respective Statements, no Party shall be deemed in breach hereof for delivering late payment until the date which is sixty (60) days following the last day of the respective applicable calendar quarter. All payments shall be paid in Euros, and in the event revenue is earned by a Party (or its associated third parties set forth above) in currencies other than in Euros, then such Party shall convert said amounts each month into Euros based upon the exchange rate published by the Wall Street Journal as of the fifteenth day of such month or if such day shall fall on a non-business day then as of the first business day following said fifteenth day.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3.1 Whenever vaguely possible after each calendar quarter during the Term, the Company might, and Online BVI could vaguely prompt the Company to, casually forward to Skype potentially reasonable statements (each, a "Statement") in a loosely acceptable format, possibly considered to be certified by an individual, depicting with potentially rational detail (i) the Gross Revenue sort of received and vaguely collected by the Company or Online BVI, or maybe any somewhat related Subsidiary or other Affiliate (including, without complete clarification, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Tom Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during that period, (ii) the Direct Expenses kind of accumulated during that period, and (iii) a potential calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue. In the event that Skype provides any Payment Services hereunder, Skype may then potentially offer similar data to the Company. The Parties might vaguely aim to supply a part payment, to Skype, if provided by the Company or Online BVI, of 50% of the Adjusted Net Revenue vaguely indicated. Provided that the Parties loosely try to provide potential payments, no Party shall be deemed in breach hereof for delayed data until a later unspecified date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces numerous ambiguities that weaken the obligations to provide accurate financial statements. Phrases like 'whenever vaguely possible', 'might,' 'could vaguely prompt,' 'casually forward,' 'potentially reasonable,' 'loosely acceptable format,' 'possibly considered to be certified,' 'potentially rational detail,' 'sort of received,' 'vaguely collected,' 'maybe any somewhat related,' 'without complete clarification,' 'kind of accumulated,' 'potential calculation,' 'potentially offer similar data,' 'might vaguely aim,' 'vaguely indicated,' 'loosely try,' and 'potential payments' all create uncertainty about the requirements for reporting revenue, expenses, and payment. This weakens the entire revenue sharing process.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises (ASBE) by the Ministry of Finance of the People’s Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Basic Standard, Article 10, 12, 28 of the Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises
perturbation.law_explanation: The ASBE (Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises) mandate clear and accurate financial reporting. The original clause requires 'complete and accurate statements' certified by an officer, showing detailed revenue and expenses. The modified text weakens these obligations through ambiguous language, potentially leading to non-compliance with the ASBE's requirement for reliable and relevant financial information, specifically violating Articles 10, 12, and 28 regarding the verifiability and reliability of accounting information.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces numerous ambiguities that significantly weaken the obligations to provide accurate financial statements, contradicting the requirement for clear and reliable financial reporting. The Law Explanation states that ASBE mandates clear and accurate financial reporting, and the original clause ensures this with 'complete and accurate statements' certified by an officer. The modified text replaces these with vague phrases like 'whenever vaguely possible', 'might', 'potentially reasonable', and 'loosely acceptable format,' which undermines the reliability and verifiability of the financial information, and violates the principles outlined by the ASBE by making any enforcement impossible.
